title: The Top 8 Herbal Tinctures for Shingles Sufferers

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol, also known as cranberry, is good for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and itching, and also boost the immune system to fight the virus that causes shingles.
Shogaol, also known as cranberry, may help shingles due to its anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and swelling caused by the shingles virus.
Zingiberene, also known as cranberry, may be good for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help reduce pain and swelling caused by the shingles virus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that can help with shingles symptoms. Shingles is a painful rash caused by the varicella-zoster virus, which can be triggered by stress, age, or a weakened immune system. Ginger tincture contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. When taken internally, these compounds can help reduce the pain and discomfort associated with shingles. Ginger tincture may also help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area. Additionally, ginger has antiviral properties, which can help to combat the varicella-zoster virus. Taking ginger tincture may also help to boost the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of shingles symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vaccinium macrocarpon tincture for shingles relief, you'll need fresh or dried cranberries (the plant's berries are known as Vaccinium macrocarpon), high-proof vodka or another neutral spirit, and a clean glass jar. First, harvest or obtain fresh Vaccinium macrocarpon berries, or use dried ones if available. Next, chop the berries and fill a clean glass jar about 2/3 full. Pour enough vodka over the berries to completely cover them, making sure all the berries are submerged. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the spirit extract the plant's healing properties. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Vaccinium macrocarpon tincture is now ready to use – mix a few drops with water or take it as directed by a healthcare professional for shingles relief.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for shingles

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides, also known as cranberry, may help alleviate shingles symptoms because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and itching caused by the shingles virus.

Cichoric acid is good for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the pain and discomfort associated with the condition by reducing swelling and easing nerve damage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture is sometimes used to help with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory properties. Shingles is a painful rash caused by a virus called herpes zoster. When this virus reactivates, it can cause a burning sensation and painful blisters on the skin. Echinacea purpurea is a plant that has been used in herbal medicine for many years. It contains compounds like alkylamides and glycosides that may help reduce inflammation and ease pain. Some people believe that taking Echinacea purpurea tincture can help relieve the symptoms of shingles, such as itching, burning, and pain. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Echinacea purpurea tincture for shingles.  When using Echinacea purpurea tincture for shingles, it's essential to follow the correct dosage instructions and start taking it early in the course of the illness. It may also be combined with other herbal remedies, such as calendula, which has anti-inflammatory properties as well. Some people choose to take Echinacea purpurea tincture topically, applying it directly to the affected skin area, but it's crucial to dilute it with a carrier oil first.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues. The tincture made from this plant is thought to be good for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. This means it can help reduce the pain, inflammation, and discomfort associated with shingles. The tincture is said to work by reducing the severity of the shingles rash and helping to heal the blisters faster. Some people also believe that it can help reduce the frequency and severity of shingles outbreaks in the long run. The active compounds in Hypericum perforatum, such as hyperforin and hypericin, are thought to be responsible for its therapeutic effects. These compounds have been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help combat the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles. When taken as a tincture, Hypericum perforatum may help alleviate the symptoms of shingles, including nerve pain, itching, and burning sensations. However, it's essential to note that more research is needed to fully understand its effects on shingles.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vaccinium macrocarpon tincture, which may help alleviate shingles symptoms, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need 1 cup of dried Vaccinium macrocarpon berries, also known as cranberries, and a clean glass jar with a lid. Combine the dried berries with 2 cups of 80- to 100-proof vodka in the jar, making sure the berries are completely covered. Secure the lid and let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process is called maceration, where the vodka helps extract the medicinal properties from the berries. After the maceration period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting liquid is your Vaccinium macrocarpon tincture. To use the tincture, mix 20-30 drops with water and take it 3-4 times a day as needed. Keep in mind that this tincture is not a substitute for medical treatment, and consult with a healthcare professional before using it to alleviate shingles symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones, also known as cranberry, have antiviral properties that may help reduce the severity and duration of shingles by inhibiting the replication of the herpes zoster virus, which is the primary cause of this condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tincture is a liquid extract made from the aloe vera plant, which has been used for centuries to heal skin problems. For shingles, aloe barbadensis tincture is good because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the swelling and pain associated with shingles. It also has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by the shingles virus. Additionally, aloe barbadensis tincture contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial properties that can help fight off the shingles virus and prevent further infection. When applied to the affected skin, the tincture can help soothe the area, reduce itching and discomfort, and promote the healing process. This can help alleviate the symptoms of shingles, such as burning, itching, and pain, and support the body's natural healing process.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal tinctures that help with shingles, you will need Vaccinium macrocarpon berries, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a clean glass jar. First, wash and dry the Vaccinium macrocarpon berries, then place them in the glass jar, filling it about 2/3 full. Next, pour the solvent over the berries, making sure they are completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the solvent extract the herbs' properties. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken in small amounts, usually 20-30 drops, 3-4 times a day, mixed with water or another liquid. You can also add the tincture to a warm bath for relief from shingles pain and discomfort. It's essential to note that Vaccinium macrocarpon tincture is not a substitute for medical treatment, but it may help alleviate symptoms and support the healing process.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tincture is a good remedy for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce pain and swelling in the affected area. The tincture contains compounds that can soothe the skin and promote healing. Aloe vera also has antiviral properties, which can help fight off the virus that causes shingles. It can also help to reduce itching and burning sensations. Additionally, aloe vera is rich in vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin A, C, and E, which can help to nourish the skin and promote recovery. By applying aloe vera tincture to the affected area, you can help to calm the symptoms of shingles and promote faster healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal tinctures that help with shingles, you will need some basic equipment and dried cranberry (Vaccinium macrocarpon) leaves. First, acquire high-quality, organic dried cranberry leaves and store them in an airtight container. Next, combine 1 part of dried cranberry leaves with 2 parts of a neutral-tasting liquid, such as vodka or glycerin, in a clean glass jar. Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for about 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day or two to help the mixture infuse. This process is called maceration, and it allows the medicinal properties of the dried cranberry leaves to be extracted into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, and discard the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) or added to water as a tea, typically in small doses (about 20-30 drops) several times a day to help alleviate shingles symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tincture is a natural remedy that may help alleviate symptoms of shingles. Shingles is a painful rash caused by the varicella-zoster virus, which can cause severe itching, burning, and blistering on the skin. Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help soothe and calm the skin. The tincture can be applied topically to the affected area to reduce pain and inflammation, and may also help to speed up the healing process. Additionally, Melissa officinalis has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that may be triggered by the pain and discomfort of shingles. Some people also find that the tincture helps to reduce the intensity and duration of shingles outbreaks, making it a popular natural remedy for those who suffer from this condition.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal tinctures that help with shingles, you'll need a few basic items. First, you'll need to obtain some Vaccinium macrocarpon, also known as cranberry, and its roots, which have antiviral properties and can help combat shingles. Once you have the ingredients, dry the roots in a cool, dark place or using a food dehydrator until they're completely dry and brittle. Next, combine 1 part of the dried roots with 2 parts of a solvent, such as vodka or glycerin, in a clean glass jar. The solvent helps extract the beneficial properties from the roots. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, shaking it well every few days for about 2-3 weeks. This process is called maceration, and it allows the solvent to extract the properties from the roots. After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Vaccinium macrocarpon tincture is now ready to use, but make sure to store it in a cool, dark place and follow the recommended dosage on the label.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture can be helpful for people with shingles because it may improve blood flow to the affected area. Shingles is caused by the varicella-zoster virus, which can cause nerve pain and discomfort. When blood flow is poor, it can make the pain worse. Ginkgo biloba has flavonoids and terpenoids that may help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. This can help alleviate the pain, itching, and tingling associated with shingles. Ginkgo biloba may also have antioxidant properties that can help protect the nerves and surrounding tissue from damage. Some people use ginkgo biloba tincture to help manage shingles symptoms, but it's essential to talk to a doctor or healthcare professional before trying any new treatment.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal tinctures that help with shingles, start by gathering fresh or dried cranberries, which is the scientific name for Vaccinium macrocarpon. You can use either the leaves, stems, or the berries of the plant, but the berries are more commonly used for medicinal purposes. First, prepare your cranberries by washing them thoroughly and then drying them, either by air-drying or using a food dehydrator. Next, combine your dried cranberries with a solvent like 80% to 90% vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar, making sure the cranberries are completely submerged in the liquid. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for at least two weeks to allow the plant's properties to infuse into the solvent. After two weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your tincture is now ready to use, but it's best to let it mature for a few more weeks before using it to help alleviate shingles symptoms.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a helpful remedy for shingles, a painful condition caused by the varicella-zoster virus. Two herbs that have shown promise in alleviating shingles symptoms are Zingiber officinale (ginger) and Echinacea purpurea. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling, while also easing nausea and digestive issues that often accompany shingles. Echinacea, on the other hand, has immune-boosting properties that can help combat the virus, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak. Fixing the issue of shingles is crucial because it can significantly impact one's quality of life, causing immense pain, discomfort, and emotional distress. It can also lead to complications such as postherpetic neuralgia, a chronic condition characterized by persistent pain. By addressing the issue through herbal tinctures, individuals can improve their life in several ways. They can reduce their reliance on medication, alleviate painful symptoms, and regain their confidence and mobility. This, in turn, can enable them to engage in activities they love, maintain their relationships, and maintain their overall well-being.
supplementary_best: For shingles remedy, a combination of Melissa officinalis (lemon balm), Hypericum perforatum (St. John's Wort), and Aloe barbadensis (aloe vera) is recommended.  Melissa officinalis has anti-viral properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of the shingles outbreak. Its soothing properties can also calm the skin, reducing pain and discomfort.  Hypericum perforatum has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity of the shingles rash and alleviate pain and discomfort. It also has antiseptic properties, which can help prevent infections.  Aloe barbadensis is known for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help calm the skin and reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles. It also has antiviral properties, which can help combat the virus that causes shingles.  This combination is effective because it addresses multiple aspects of shingles, including pain, inflammation, and viral activity. The anti-inflammatory properties of Hypericum perforatum and Aloe barbadensis can help reduce pain and swelling, while Melissa officinalis can help combat the virus and reduce the severity of the outbreak.  To use this combination, take 20 drops of each tincture three times a day. You can also apply Aloe barbadensis topically to the affected area to get relief from pain and inflammation.
supplementary_avoid: If you have shingles, it's best to avoid certain herbal tinctures.  St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum) tincture is not recommended because it may interact with antiviral medications and reduce their effectiveness.  Valerian root (Valeriana officinalis) tincture may make shingles symptoms worse because it can cause drowsiness and slow down the healing process.  Ginseng tinctures (Panax spp.), especially American ginseng, can weaken the immune system, making shingles worse.  Lavender (Lavandula angustifolia) tincture may cause skin irritation, which can worsen shingles symptoms.  Skullcap (Scutellaria baicalensis) tincture, while used to reduce pain, may interact with pain medications and worsen shingles symptoms.  Slippery elm (Ulmus rubra) tincture may interact with antiviral medications and reduce their effectiveness.  Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra) tincture can weaken the immune system, making shingles worse.  Comfrey (Symphytum officinale) tincture may slow down the healing process.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel can help soothe the itchy and painful skin of shingles. It has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling, making it easier for the skin to heal. You can apply aloe vera gel directly to the affected area several times a day.  Tea tree oil has antiviral properties that may help fight off the shingles virus. However, it's essential to mix it with a carrier oil like coconut oil and apply it to the skin in a thin layer, as it can be quite strong.  Oatmeal baths can provide relief from the itching and burning sensation associated with shingles. Oatmeal has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can calm the skin. You can add colloidal oatmeal to warm bath water and soak in it for 15-20 minutes.  Epsom salt baths can also help with shingles. Epsom salt contains magnesium, which helps reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and improve blood flow to the affected area. Soaking in an Epsom salt bath can provide temporary relief from the pain and discomfort of shingles.  Topical vitamin E oil may help reduce the severity and duration of shingles by promoting skin healing and reducing scarring. It also has antioxidant properties that can protect the skin from further damage.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have shingles may experience other health issues due to the virus causing shingles, the varicella-zoster virus. This virus can cause inflammation and pain in the body. Some common ailments people with shingles may experience include:  - Nerve pain: Shingles is caused by a reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus in the nerves. This nerve pain can be severe and is often described as burning or shooting. Medicinal herbs like ashwagandha and passionflower can help reduce nerve pain due to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.  - Anxiety and stress: The pain and discomfort caused by shingles can lead to feelings of anxiety and stress. Medicinal herbs like lavender and chamomile have a calming effect on the body and can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.  - Weakened immune system: The varicella-zoster virus can weaken the immune system, making it harder for the body to fight off infections. Medicinal herbs like echinacea and ginseng can help boost the immune system and help the body fight off infections.  - Skin issues: The rash caused by shingles can lead to skin issues like dry skin, itching, and skin infections. Medicinal herbs like aloe vera and tea tree oil can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing dryness and itching.  - Fatigue: The pain and discomfort caused by shingles can lead to fatigue and exhaustion. Medicinal herbs like ginkgo biloba and ashwagandha can help improve energy levels and reduce fatigue.  - Digestive issues: The stress and anxiety caused by shingles can lead to digestive issues like constipation, diarrhea, and stomach pain. Medicinal herbs like peppermint and ginger can help soothe the digestive system and reduce symptoms of digestive issues.
